items.title: Chinese exile once linked to Trump strategist gets 30-year sentence in $1 billion fraud
items.description: The businessman known as Miles Guo, who pushed the fraudulent H-Coin project and had close ties to Steve Bannon, was finally sentenced after his 2024 conviction.

items.title: Jefferies warns of crypto market volatility as Clarity Act faces Senate test
items.description: The bank said the bill faces major hurdles, with passage boosting institutional crypto adoption and delays prolonging regulatory uncertainty.

items.title: Circle slides 13% as Stripe, Coinbase and BlackRock back rival stablecoin network
items.description: Open Standard's Open USD aims to let partners keep reserve income and eliminate minting fees, challenging Circle's USDC.

items.title: MetaMask launches Money Account with stablecoin yield and spending in one wallet
items.description: The launch reflects a broader push to make stablecoins more useful beyond trading and transfers.

items.title: Tokenized securities need competition, not gatekeepers
items.description: Innovation thrives when investors have choices. For tokenized securities, Washington shouldn't pick winners before the market has a chance to learn what works, argues Patrick McHenry, vice chairman at Ondo Finance and former Chairman of the House Financial Services Committee.

items.title: Nasdaq expands distribution of its market data into blockchain infrastructure
items.description: The exchange operator will offer its TotalView data feed through Pyth's marketplace as financial firms increasingly build applications on blockchain rails.

items.title: Bitcoin’s quiet $59,000-$60,000 range is starting to look dangerous
items.description: The token has traded in a tight band near $59,000 to $60,000 all week. The pattern echoes a calm stretch from 2024, but this one is forming below support in a falling market, and a break could open the way toward $40,000.

items.title: New York Life's $800 billion asset manager makes tokenization debut with Centrifuge fund
items.description: The $807 billion asset manager is bringing a high-yield corporate bond strategy onto blockchain with Centrifuge as Wall Street expands beyond tokenized Treasury funds.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: MetaMask launches stablecoin yield account with card spending
items.description: MetaMask launches Money Account it says offers up to 4% variable APY on mUSD stablecoin balances and card spending, with DeFi-powered yield via vaults, excluding the UK and EU.

items.title: OKX launches AI marketplace for autonomous agent economy
items.description: OKX launched a beta marketplace for the agentic economy, enabling AI agents to autonomously find work and collaborate with other agents.

items.title: Strategy's new plan divides industry observers even as MSTR, STRC climb
items.description: Strategy’s new Bitcoin capital framework draws Wall Street backing from Benchmark with a $570 per share target even as traders question long-term demand risk.

items.title: Theo becomes first crypto-native investor in Fidelity tokenized fund
items.description: The onchain capital markets platform allocated $20 million to Fidelity International's tokenized liquidity fund as tokenized Treasury products continue to attract institutional capital.

items.title: Swan's Cory Klippsten sees record Bitcoin holder supply revealing early bottom
items.description: The record Bitcoin supply held by long-term holders may suggest that the crypto market bottom will come early, according to Swan’s Cory Klippsten.

items.title: StarkWare unveils Starknet quantum roadmap, says industry has no excuse
items.description: “The crypto industry shouldn’t need wake-up calls from the White House or anyone else,” said StarkWare CEO Eli Ben-Sasson.

items.title: Spiko links EU regulated T-bill funds to Coinbase stablecoin rails
items.description: Spiko integrated Coinbase Payments into two EU regulated UCITS Treasury funds, enabling USDC and EURC subscriptions and redemption payments through Base.

items.title: AWS puts $1 billion into new AI unit to embed engineers with customers, joining growing wave
items.description: AWS FDEs will look to leave behind self-sufficient teams with new AI solutions and capabilities in a matter of weeks, the company said.

items.title: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, blocks Trump order
items.description: President Donald Trump attended oral arguments in the case, underscoring his staunch opposition to granting automatic citizenship to many immigrants' babies.

items.title: Nike is set to report earnings after the bell. Here's what to expect
items.description: Nike is expected to report another quarter of declining sales as the sneaker giant struggles to regain strength under its turnaround strategy.

items.title: Blue Origin pivots to redesigned launchpad after explosion in push to fly by end of 2026
items.description: Blue Origin won't be "rebuilding the same pad," and will instead adopt a configuration that was in development for a larger variant of its New Glenn rocket.

items.title: Cleveland Fed President Hammack sees AI fueling inflation, says rate hikes may be necessary
items.description: "We've got inflation that's too high, and it's been too high for the past five years," Beth Hammack told CNBC's Sara Eisen.

items.title: Small-cap stocks enjoy their best first half in 35 years. Here's what's driving it
items.description: The advance marks a sharp turnaround after years of underperformance versus large-cap peers.

items.title: Oil prices set for steep monthly drop as Trump, Iran issue mixed messages on talks in Qatar
items.description: The moves come as oil traders monitor the prospect of U.S.-Iran talks in Doha on Tuesday.

items.title: Do you really, really love your job? Then you're not alone, according to surprising results from this survey
items.description: Those surveyed showed a 78.9% rate of workers who "reported feeling positive at the end of their shifts."

items.description: Ruling is major blow to president’s immigration agenda

items.title: The great wealth transfer rattling Wall Street
items.description: Tens of trillions of dollars are moving between generations — and younger heirs feel little loyalty to traditional advisers

items.title: America’s stillborn 250th birthday
items.description: An event that was meant to celebrate an exceptional nation has been cornered by one man

items.title: Elliott’s acolytes: how Paul Singer’s hedge fund became a spinout factory
items.description: The vaunted investor has spawned a small army of new firms mirroring Julian Robertson’s ‘Tiger cubs’

items.title: Gold heads for worst quarter in more than a decade as retail frenzy fades
items.description: Expectations of higher interest rates fuelled by Iran war help end bullion’s record rally

items.title: Ukrainian tycoon injured in Monaco bomb attack
items.description: Police hunt suspected bomber believed to have fled wealthy Mediterranean principality

items.description: Kyiv seeks to justify strikes on tankers after Russia accuses it of ‘terrorism’ in letter to IMO

items.description: Aleksandar Vučić says he plans to step down as president and return as premier after snap elections this autumn

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Wf5bKnlOpd0! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Sandisk’s stock could follow record-breaking quarter with another big jump, analysts say
items.description: As investors get more discerning in separating potential artificial-intelligence winners from losers, memory stocks have become a standout trade.

items.title: ‘She is retired’: Do I dip into my 401(k) to pay my mother’s $30,000 credit-card debt?
items.description: “I want her to live on her Social Security instead of using it to pay off her credit-card debt.”

items.title: ‘I claimed Social Security at 62’: At 76, I’m working at Walmart. Why do I still owe payroll taxes?
items.description: “It seems like half of the workforce at our local Walmart is over 65.”

items.title: Even Wall Street bulls are worried about a stock-market pullback after dazzling second quarter
items.description: The first half of 2026 is coming to a close on Tuesday, and with it, another rollicking stretch for the U.S. equity market.

items.title: Job openings rise to 2-year high, but good luck actually getting one
items.description: A prolonged decline in job openings in the U.S. appears to be over, but it doesn’t mean businesses are ready to do lots of hiring or make it easier for people to find work.

items.title: Alphabet’s stock slump is a ‘tactical buying opportunity,’ according to one analyst
items.description: Investors are overlooking the potential of Alphabet’s custom-chip business as the company plans to expand its data-center capacity going into 2028, according to Morgan Stanley.

items.title: Insurers shifted roof-replacement costs onto homeowners thanks to a new federal rule — just in time for hail and hurricane season
items.description: Those who find extensive damage to their roofs might be stuck between two options: File an insurance claim, which could trigger a premium increase, or cough up the cash for the necessary repairs or replacement.

items.title: What’s a ‘soft’ off day? How to take one without getting in trouble at work.
items.description: Plus: Why you should consider taking a “life admin” day.

items.title: Can Kazakhstan’s oil boom survive Putin’s War? | FT Film

items.title: US Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to end birthright citizenship
items.description: Ruling is major blow to president’s immigration agenda

items.title: Starmer’s £15bn defence plan leaves short-term gaps, say former officers
items.description: UK’s large military investments are aimed at countering China, but questions remain about ability to withstand Russian threat

items.title: Shetland Islands to be connected by tunnels to try to boost growth
items.description: The four road links will eventually replace ageing ferry fleets

items.title: UK weighs intervention in Paramount’s $111bn Warner Bros takeover
items.description: Any regulatory referral on plurality grounds could delay or halt proceedings

items.title: US labour demand remained firm in May
items.description: Latest Jolts survey reinforces narratives of robust labour market

items.title: German leftwing terrorism on the rise, spy agency warns
items.description: Increase in violent incidents comes in response to far-right extremism and Israeli actions in Middle East

items.title: Britain has an Oxbridge innovation problem
items.description: Universities challenged

items.title: Dressing for a warming world at Paris Men’s Fashion Week
items.description: As a brutal heatwave broke French records, designers offered ideas on how to look cool — with mixed degrees of success

items.title: NPR Retracts Article That Mistakenly Said Justice Alito Would Retire
items.description: The news outlet quickly removed the article, which was written by NPR’s seasoned Supreme Court reporter, Nina Totenberg, from its website.

items.title: BMW Will Build a New Electric S.U.V. in South Carolina
items.description: The German company is doubling down on electric vehicles even as other automakers pull back after acknowledging billions of dollars in losses.

items.title: Nursing Students and Others Get Higher Student Loan Limits
items.description: A legal challenge to a law that takes effect this week has doubled the amount certain graduate students can borrow from the federal government to $50,000 per year.

items.title: How the Supreme Court Ushered in Corporate Chaos in D.C.
items.description: Though the high court affirmed the political independence of the Federal Reserve, it gave the presidency huge control over dozens of federal regulators.

items.title: Prominent Tech Founder in Indonesia Is Found Guilty in Corruption Case
items.description: The case against Nadiem Makarim, a co-founder of Gojek, has fueled concerns about judicial fairness in a nation where foreign investors were already growing wary.

items.title: Shipping Traffic in Strait of Hormuz Picks Up Again
items.description: Vessels stranded for months have started moving in larger numbers, but many pulled back over the weekend after Iran and the U.S. exchanged attacks.

items.title: Oil Prices Tick Higher as Iran and U.S. Prepare for Talks
items.description: Iran and the United States were set for further talks after a spurt of hostilities had underscored the uncertainty surrounding attempts to strike a lasting peace deal.

items.title: How Grindr’s C.E.O. Adopted A.I.: ‘I Just Imposed It’
items.description: George Arison, the gay dating app’s chief executive, is aiming for all code to be eventually written by artificial intelligence, making the company “leaner.”
